text: Alif-L√£m-Mń©m.
Allah! There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him‚ÄĒthe Ever-Living, All-Sustaining.
He has revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ the Book in truth, confirming what came before it, as He revealed the Torah and the Gospel

previously, as a guide for people, and ňĻalsoňļ revealed the Standard ňĻto distinguish between right and wrongňļ. <sup foot_note=120>120</sup> Surely those who reject Allah‚Äôs revelations will suffer a severe torment. For Allah is Almighty, capable of punishment.

foot_notes.120: The Standard ( Al-Furq√Ęn ) is one of the names of the Quran.

text: Surely nothing on earth or in the heavens is hidden from Allah.
He is the One Who shapes you in the wombs of your mothers as He wills. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise.

He is the One Who has revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ the Book, of which some verses are precise‚ÄĒthey are the foundation of the Book‚ÄĒwhile others are elusive. <sup foot_note=121>121</sup> Those with deviant hearts follow the elusive verses seeking ňĻto spreadňļ doubt through their ňĻfalseňļ interpretations‚ÄĒbut none grasps their ňĻfullňļ meaning except Allah. As for those well-grounded in knowledge, they say, ‚ÄúWe believe in this ňĻQuranňļ‚ÄĒit is all from our Lord.‚ÄĚ But none will be mindful ňĻof thisňļ except people of reason.

foot_notes.121: The precise verses are easy to understand and deal mostly with matters of belief and practice, such as the belief in one God ( S√Ľrah 112), forbidden foods ( see 5:3), and the commandments in 6:151-152 and 17:23-39. As for the elusive verses, their full meaning is known only to Allah, such as the meaning of letter combinations at the beginning of some s√Ľrahs , such as Alif-L√£m-Mń©m ( see Stylistic Features), and how Allah settles Himself on the Throne.

text: ňĻThey say,ňļ ‚ÄúOur Lord! Do not let our hearts deviate after you have guided us. Grant us Your mercy. You are indeed the Giver ňĻof all bountiesňļ.
Our Lord! You will certainly gather all humanity for the ňĻpromisedňļ Day‚ÄĒabout which there is no doubt. Surely Allah does not break His promise.‚ÄĚ
Indeed, neither the wealth nor children <sup foot_note=122>122</sup> of the disbelievers will be of any benefit to them against Allah‚ÄĒand they will be the fuel for the Fire.

foot_notes.122: Wealth and children were considered the most important things in pre-Islamic Arabia, and so the frequent mention of these things in the Quran is meant to represent all worldly gains.

text: Their fate will be like that of the people of Pharaoh and those before them‚ÄĒthey all rejected Our signs, so Allah seized them for their sins. And Allah is severe in punishment.
ňĻO Prophet!ňļ Tell the disbelievers, ‚ÄúSoon you will be overpowered and driven to Hell‚ÄĒwhat an evil place to rest!‚ÄĚ

Indeed, there was a sign for you in the two armies that met in battle‚ÄĒone fighting for the cause of Allah and the other in denial. The believers saw their enemy twice their number. <sup foot_note=124>124</sup> But Allah supports with His victory whoever He wills. Surely in this is a lesson for people of insight.

foot_notes.124: It can also be understood that the disbelievers were made to think there were twice as many believers in the battlefield.

text: The enjoyment of ňĻworldlyňļ desires‚ÄĒwomen, children, <sup foot_note=125>125</sup> treasures of gold and silver, fine horses, cattle, and fertile land‚ÄĒhas been made appealing to people. These are the pleasures of this worldly life, but with Allah is the finest destination.

foot_notes.125: Ban√ģn means sons. In the ancient Arab culture, sons were a source of pride for their parents and tribes. This is because they provided for their families and took up arms in defence of their tribes.

text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúShall I inform you of what is better than ňĻall ofňļ this? Those mindful ňĻof Allahňļ will have Gardens with their Lord under which rivers flow, to stay there forever, and pure spouses, <sup foot_note=126>126</sup> along with Allah‚Äôs pleasure.‚ÄĚ And Allah is All-Seeing of ňĻHisňļ servants,
who pray, ‚ÄúOur Lord! We have believed, so forgive our sins and protect us from the torment of the Fire.‚ÄĚ
ňĻIt is theyňļ who are patient, sincere, obedient, and charitable, and who pray for forgiveness before dawn. <sup foot_note=127>127</sup>

foot_notes.127: Optional prayers before dawn are recommended and are more likely to be accepted.

text: Allah ňĻHimselfňļ is a Witness that there is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him‚ÄĒand so are the angels and people of knowledge. He is the Maintainer of justice. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise.

Certainly, Allah‚Äôs only Way is Islam. <sup foot_note=128>128</sup> Those who were given the Scripture did not dispute ňĻamong themselvesňļ out of mutual envy until knowledge came to them. <sup foot_note=129>129</sup> Whoever denies Allah‚Äôs signs, then surely Allah is swift in reckoning.

foot_notes.128: i.e., full submission to the Will of Allah.
foot_notes.129: No community split into believers and disbelievers until they received the knowledge given by their prophet.

text: So if they argue with you ňĻO Prophetňļ, say, ‚ÄúI have submitted myself to Allah, and so have my followers.‚ÄĚ And ask those who were given the Scripture and the illiterate ňĻpeopleňļ, <sup foot_note=130>130</sup> ‚ÄúHave you submitted yourselves ňĻto Allahňļ?‚ÄĚ If they submit, they will be ňĻrightlyňļ guided. But if they turn away, then your duty is only to deliver ňĻthe messageňļ. And Allah is All-Seeing of ňĻHisňļ servants.

foot_notes.130: This refers to the pagans of Arabia before Islam.

text: Indeed, those who deny Allah‚Äôs signs, kill the prophets unjustly, and kill people who stand up for justice‚ÄĒgive them good news of a painful punishment.
They are the ones whose deeds are wasted in this world and the Hereafter. And they will have no helpers.

Have you not seen those who were given a portion of the Scriptures? <sup foot_note=131>131</sup> Yet when they are invited to the Book of Allah to settle their disputes, some of them turn away heedlessly.
This is because they say, ‚ÄúThe Fire will not touch us except for a few days.‚ÄĚ They have been deceived in their faith by their wishful lying.
But how ňĻhorribleňļ will it be when We gather them together on the Day about which there is no doubt‚ÄĒwhen every soul will be paid in full for what it has done, and none will be wronged!

Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO Allah! Lord over all authorities! You give authority to whoever You please and remove it from who You please; You honour whoever You please and disgrace who You please‚ÄĒall good is in Your Hands. Surely You ňĻaloneňļ are Most Capable of everything.
You cause the night to pass into the day and the day into the night. You bring forth the living from the dead and the dead from the living. And You provide for whoever You will without limit.‚ÄĚ

Believers should not take disbelievers as guardians instead of the believers‚ÄĒand whoever does so will have nothing to hope for from Allah‚ÄĒunless it is a precaution against their tyranny. And Allah warns you about Himself. And to Allah is the final return.

Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWhether you conceal what is in your hearts or reveal it, it is known to Allah. For He knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And Allah is Most Capable of everything.‚ÄĚ

ňĻWatch forňļ the Day when every soul will be presented with whatever good it has done. And it will wish that its misdeeds were far off. And Allah warns you about Himself. And Allah is Ever Gracious to ňĻHisňļ servants.
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIf you ňĻsincerelyňļ love Allah, then follow me; Allah will love you and forgive your sins. For Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.‚ÄĚ
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúObey Allah and His Messenger.‚ÄĚ If they still turn away, then truly Allah does not like the disbelievers.
Indeed, Allah chose Adam, Noah, the family of Abraham, and the family of ‚ÄôImr√Ęn above all people ňĻof their timeňļ.
They are descendants of one another. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

ňĻRememberňļ when the wife of ‚ÄôImr√Ęn said, ‚ÄúMy Lord! I dedicate what is in my womb entirely to Your service, <sup foot_note=133>133</sup> so accept it from me. You ňĻaloneňļ are truly the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.133: i.e., dedicating the child to service in the temple.

text: When she delivered, she said, ‚ÄúMy Lord! I have given birth to a girl,‚ÄĚ‚ÄĒand Allah fully knew what she had delivered‚ÄĒ‚Äúand the male is not like the female. <sup foot_note=134>134</sup> I have named her Mary, and I seek Your protection for her and her offspring from Satan, the accursed.‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=135>135</sup>

foot_notes.134: Service at the temple was hosted by males only.
foot_notes.135: The prayers of Mary‚Äôs mother were answered. In a Šł•ad√ģth collected by Bukh√Ęri and Muslim, the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) says, ‚ÄúEvery child is touched by Satan when they are born‚ÄĒand they cry because of this contact‚ÄĒexcept Jesus and his mother.‚ÄĚ

text: So her Lord accepted her graciously and blessed her with a pleasant upbringing‚ÄĒentrusting her to the care of Zachariah. Whenever Zachariah visited her in the sanctuary, he found her supplied with provisions. He exclaimed, ‚ÄúO Mary! Where did this come from?‚ÄĚ She replied, ‚ÄúIt is from Allah. Surely Allah provides for whoever He wills without limit.‚ÄĚ
Then and there Zachariah prayed to his Lord, saying, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Grant me‚ÄĒby your grace‚ÄĒrighteous offspring. You are certainly the Hearer of ňĻallňļ prayers.‚ÄĚ

So the angels called out to him while he stood praying in the sanctuary, ‚ÄúAllah gives you good news of ňĻthe birth ofňļ John who will confirm the Word of Allah and will be a great leader, chaste, and a prophet among the righteous.‚ÄĚ
Zachariah exclaimed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! How can I have a son when I am very old and my wife is barren?‚ÄĚ He replied, ‚ÄúSo will it be. Allah does what He wills.‚ÄĚ

Zachariah said, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Grant me a sign.‚ÄĚ He said, ‚ÄúYour sign is that you will not ňĻbe able toňļ speak to people for three days except through gestures. Remember your Lord often and glorify ňĻHimňļ morning and evening.‚ÄĚ
And ňĻrememberňļ when the angels said, ‚ÄúO Mary! Surely Allah has selected you, purified you, and chosen you over all women of the world.
O Mary! Be devout to your Lord, prostrate yourself ňĻin prayerňļ and bow along with those who bow down.‚ÄĚ
This is news of the unseen that We reveal to you ňĻO Prophetňļ. You were not with them when they cast lots to decide who would be Mary‚Äôs guardian, nor were you there when they argued ňĻabout itňļ.

ňĻRememberňļ when the angels proclaimed, ‚ÄúO Mary! Allah gives you good news of a Word <sup foot_note=136>136</sup> from Him, his name will be the Messiah, <sup foot_note=137>137</sup> Jesus, son of Mary; honoured in this world and the Hereafter, and he will be one of those nearest ňĻto Allahňļ.

foot_notes.136: In the Quran, Jesus is called the ‚ÄúWord‚ÄĚ of Allah since he was created with the word ‚ÄúBe!‚ÄĚ
foot_notes.137: Messiah ( Mas√ģŠł• ) means the ‚Äúanointed one.‚ÄĚ It is used in the Quran exclusively as a title for Jesus Christ.

text: And he will speak to people in ňĻhisňļ infancy and adulthood and will be one of the righteous.‚ÄĚ

Mary wondered, ‚ÄúMy Lord! How can I have a child when no man has ever touched me?‚ÄĚ An angel replied, ‚ÄúSo will it be. Allah creates what He wills. When He decrees a matter, He simply tells it, ‚ÄėBe!‚Äô And it is!
‚ÄúAnd Allah will teach him writing and wisdom, the Torah and the Gospel,

and ňĻmake himňļ a messenger to the Children of Israel ňĻto proclaim,ňļ ‚ÄėI have come to you with a sign from your Lord: I will make for you a bird from clay, breathe into it, and it will become a ňĻrealňļ bird‚ÄĒby Allah‚Äôs Will. I will heal the blind and the leper and raise the dead to life‚ÄĒby Allah‚Äôs Will. And I will prophesize what you eat and store in your houses. Surely in this is a sign for you if you ňĻtrulyňļ believe.
And I will confirm the Torah revealed before me and legalize some of what had been forbidden to you. I have come to you with a sign from your Lord, so be mindful of Allah and obey me.
Surely Allah is my Lord and your Lord. So worship Him ňĻaloneňļ. This is the Straight Path.‚Äô‚ÄĚ

When Jesus sensed disbelief from his people, he asked, ‚ÄúWho will stand up with me for Allah?‚ÄĚ The disciples replied, ‚ÄúWe will stand up for Allah. We believe in Allah, so bear witness that we have submitted.‚ÄĚ
ňĻThey prayed to Allah,ňļ ‚ÄúOur Lord! We believe in Your revelations and follow the messenger, so count us among those who bear witness.‚ÄĚ
And the disbelievers made a plan ňĻagainst Jesusňļ, but Allah also planned‚ÄĒand Allah is the best of planners.

ňĻRememberňļ when Allah said, ‚ÄúO Jesus! I will take you <sup foot_note=138>138</sup> and raise you up to Myself. I will deliver you from those who disbelieve, and elevate your followers above the disbelievers until the Day of Judgment. Then to Me you will ňĻallňļ return, and I will settle all your disputes.

foot_notes.138: ‚Äė Mutawaff√ģka ‚Äô means literally: ‚ÄúI will make you reach the end of your worldly term.‚ÄĚ The popular Muslim belief is that a conspiracy was made to kill Jesus. Allah made the main culprit who betrayed Jesus look exactly like Jesus, while the culprit was crucified. Similar to Christians, Muslims believe in the second coming of Jesus ( Ô∑ļ ) .

text: ‚ÄúAs for those who disbelieve, I will subject them to a severe punishment in this life and the Hereafter, and they will have no helpers.
And as for those who believe and do good, they will be rewarded in full. And Allah does not like the wrongdoers.‚ÄĚ
We recite ňĻallňļ this to you ňĻO Prophetňļ as one of the signs <sup foot_note=139>139</sup> and ňĻasňļ a wise reminder.

foot_notes.139: As a testament to your prophethood.

text: Indeed, the example of Jesus in the sight of Allah is like that of Adam. He created him from dust, then said to him, ‚ÄúBe!‚ÄĚ And he was!
This is the truth from your Lord, so do not be one of those who doubt.

Now, whoever disputes with you ňĻO Prophetňļ concerning Jesus after full knowledge has come to you, say, ‚ÄúCome! Let us gather our children and your children, our women and your women, ourselves and yourselves‚ÄĒthen let us sincerely invoke Allah‚Äôs curse upon the liars.‚ÄĚ
Certainly, this is the true narrative, and there is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Allah. And indeed, Allah ňĻaloneňļ is the Almighty, All-Wise.
If they turn away, then surely Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of the corruptors.

Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO People of the Book! Let us come to common terms: that we will worship none but Allah, associate none with Him, nor take one another as lords instead of Allah.‚ÄĚ But if they turn away, then say, ‚ÄúBear witness that we have submitted ňĻto Allah aloneňļ.‚ÄĚ
O People of the Book! Why do you argue about Abraham, while the Torah and the Gospel were not revealed until long after him? Do you not understand?

Here you are! You disputed about what you have ňĻlittleňļ knowledge of, <sup foot_note=140>140</sup> but why do you now argue about what you have no knowledge of? <sup foot_note=141>141</sup> Allah knows and you do not know.

foot_notes.141: Abraham ( Ô∑ļ ) being Jewish or Christian.

text: Abraham was neither a Jew nor a Christian; he submitted in all uprightness <sup foot_note=142>142</sup> and was not a polytheist.

foot_notes.142: Linguistically, ‚ÄúMuslim‚ÄĚ means ‚Äúsomeone who has submitted themselves to Allah.‚ÄĚ

text: Indeed, those who have the best claim to Abraham are his followers, this Prophet, <sup foot_note=143>143</sup> and the believers. And Allah is the Guardian of those who believe.
Some of the People of the Book wish to mislead you ňĻbelieversňļ. They mislead none but themselves, yet they fail to perceive it.
O People of the Book! Why do you reject the signs of Allah while you bear witness ňĻto their truthňļ?
O People of the Book! Why do you mix the truth with falsehood and hide the truth knowingly?
A group among the People of the Book said ňĻto one anotherňļ, ‚ÄúBelieve in what has been revealed to the believers in the morning and reject it in the evening, so they may abandon their faith.

And only believe those who follow your religion.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúSurely, ňĻthe onlyňļ true guidance is Allah‚Äôs guidance.‚ÄĚ ňĻThey also said,ňļ ‚ÄúDo not believe that someone will receive ňĻrevealedňļ knowledge similar to yours or argue against you before your Lord.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIndeed, all bounty is in the Hands of Allah‚ÄĒHe grants it to whoever He wills. And Allah is All-Bountiful, All-Knowing.‚ÄĚ
He chooses whoever He wills to receive His mercy. And Allah is the Lord of infinite bounty.

There are some among the People of the Book who, if entrusted with a stack of gold, will readily return it. Yet there are others who, if entrusted with a single coin, will not repay it unless you constantly demand it. This is because they say, ‚ÄúWe are not accountable for ňĻexploitingňļ the Gentiles.‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=144>144</sup> And ňĻsoňļ they attribute lies to Allah knowingly.

foot_notes.144: ‚ÄúGentiles‚ÄĚ here refers to the pagans of Arabia.

text: Absolutely! Those who honour their trusts and shun evil‚ÄĒsurely Allah loves those who are mindful ňĻof Himňļ.

Indeed, those who trade Allah‚Äôs covenant and their oaths for a fleeting gain will have no share in the Hereafter. Allah will neither speak to them, nor look at them, nor purify them on the Day of Judgment. And they will suffer a painful punishment.

There are some among them who distort the Book with their tongues to make you think this ňĻdistortionňļ is from the Book‚ÄĒbut it is not what the Book says. They say, ‚ÄúIt is from Allah‚ÄĚ‚ÄĒbut it is not from Allah. And ňĻsoňļ they attribute lies to Allah knowingly.

It is not appropriate for someone who Allah has blessed with the Scripture, wisdom, and prophethood to say to people, ‚ÄúWorship me instead of Allah.‚ÄĚ Rather, he would say, ‚ÄúBe devoted to the worship of your Lord ňĻaloneňļ‚ÄĚ‚ÄĒin accordance with what these prophets read in the Scripture and what they taught.
And he would never ask you to take angels and prophets as lords. Would he ask you to disbelieve after you have submitted?

ňĻRememberňļ when Allah made a covenant with the prophets, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúNow that I have given you the Book and wisdom, if there comes to you a messenger <sup foot_note=145>145</sup> confirming what you have, you must believe in him and support him.‚ÄĚ He added, ‚ÄúDo you affirm this covenant and accept this commitment?‚ÄĚ They said, ‚ÄúYes, we do.‚ÄĚ Allah said, ‚ÄúThen bear witness, and I too am a Witness.‚ÄĚ
Whoever turns back after this, they will be the rebellious.
Do they desire a way other than Allah‚Äôs‚ÄĒknowing that all those in the heavens and the earth submit to His Will, willingly or unwillingly, and to Him they will ňĻallňļ be returned?

Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWe believe in Allah and what has been revealed to us and what was revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and his descendants; and what was given to Moses, Jesus, and other prophets from their Lord‚ÄĒwe make no distinction between any of them, and to Him we ňĻfullyňļ submit.‚ÄĚ
Whoever seeks a way other than Islam, <sup foot_note=146>146</sup> it will never be accepted from them, and in the Hereafter they will be among the losers.

foot_notes.146: i.e., full submission to the Will of Allah.

text: How will Allah guide a people who chose to disbelieve after they had believed, acknowledged the Messenger to be true, and received clear proofs? For Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.
Their reward is that they will be condemned by Allah, the angels, and all of humanity.
They will be in Hell forever. Their punishment will not be lightened, nor will they be delayed ňĻfrom itňļ.
As for those who repent afterwards and mend their ways, then surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
Indeed, those who disbelieve after having believed then increase in disbelief, their repentance will never be accepted. <sup foot_note=147>147</sup> It is they who are astray.

foot_notes.147: Their repentance will not be accepted if they die as disbelievers.

text: Indeed, if each of those who disbelieve then die as disbelievers were to offer a ransom of enough gold to fill the whole world, it would never be accepted from them. It is they who will suffer a painful punishment, and they will have no helpers.
You will never achieve righteousness until you donate some of what you cherish. And whatever you give is certainly well known to Allah.

All food was lawful for the children of Israel, except what Israel <sup foot_note=148>148</sup> made unlawful for himself before the Torah was revealed. <sup foot_note=149>149</sup> Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúBring the Torah and read it, if your claims are true.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.149: When Jacob fell sick, he made camel meat unlawful for himself, but not for the rest of his people.

text: Then whoever still fabricates lies about Allah, they will be the ňĻtrueňļ wrongdoers.
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúAllah has declared the truth. So follow the Way of Abraham, the upright‚ÄĒwho was not a polytheist.‚ÄĚ
Surely the first House ňĻof worshipňļ established for humanity is the one at Bakkah‚ÄĒa blessed sanctuary and a guide for ňĻallňļ people.

In it are clear signs and the standing-place of Abraham. Whoever enters it should be safe. Pilgrimage to this House is an obligation by Allah upon whoever is able among the people. <sup foot_note=150>150</sup> And whoever disbelieves, then surely Allah is not in need of ňĻany of Hisňļ creation.

foot_notes.150: Pilgrimage is obligatory on every Muslim at least once in their lifetime if the person is physically and financially able.

text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO People of the Book! Why do you deny the revelations of Allah, when Allah is a Witness to what you do?‚ÄĚ

Say, ‚ÄúO People of the Book! Why do you turn the believers away from the Way of Allah‚ÄĒstriving to make it ňĻappearňļ crooked, while you are witnesses ňĻto its truthňļ? And Allah is never unaware of what you do.‚ÄĚ
O believers! If you were to yield to a group of those who were given the Scripture, they would turn you back from belief to disbelief.
How can you disbelieve when Allah‚Äôs revelations are recited to you and His Messenger is in your midst? Whoever holds firmly to Allah is surely guided to the Straight Path.
O believers! Be mindful of Allah in the way He deserves, <sup foot_note=151>151</sup> and do not die except in ňĻa state of fullňļ submission ňĻto Himňļ. <sup foot_note=152>152</sup>

foot_notes.151: Meaning, always be conscious of Him; obedient and grateful to Him.

text: And hold firmly to the rope of Allah <sup foot_note=153>153</sup> and do not be divided. Remember Allah‚Äôs favour upon you when you were enemies, then He united your hearts, so you‚ÄĒby His grace‚ÄĒbecame brothers. And you were at the brink of a fiery pit and He saved you from it. This is how Allah makes His revelations clear to you, so that you may be ňĻrightlyňļ guided.
Let there be a group among you who call ňĻothersňļ to goodness, encourage what is good, and forbid what is evil‚ÄĒit is they who will be successful.
And do not be like those who split ňĻinto sectsňļ and differed after clear proofs had come to them. It is they who will suffer a tremendous punishment.
On that Day some faces will be bright while others gloomy. To the gloomy-faced it will be said, ‚ÄúDid you disbelieve after having believed? So taste the punishment for your disbelief.‚ÄĚ
As for the bright-faced, they will be in Allah‚Äôs mercy, where they will remain forever.
These are Allah‚Äôs revelations We recite to you ňĻO Prophetňļ in truth. And Allah desires no injustice to ňĻHisňļ creation.
To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And to Allah ňĻallňļ matters will be returned ňĻfor judgmentňļ.

You are the best community ever raised for humanity‚ÄĒyou encourage good, forbid evil, and believe in Allah. Had the People of the Book believed, it would have been better for them. Some of them are faithful, but most are rebellious.
They can never inflict harm on you, except a little annoyance. <sup foot_note=154>154</sup> But if they meet you in battle, they will flee and they will have no helpers.

foot_notes.154: i.e., they can only annoy you with their words.

text: They will be stricken with disgrace wherever they go, unless they are protected by a covenant with Allah or a treaty with the people. They have invited the displeasure of Allah and have been branded with misery for rejecting Allah‚Äôs revelations and murdering ňĻHisňļ prophets unjustly. This is ňĻa fair rewardňļ for their disobedience and violations.
Yet they are not all alike: there are some among the People of the Book who are upright, who recite Allah‚Äôs revelations throughout the night, prostrating ňĻin prayerňļ.
They believe in Allah and the Last Day, encourage good and forbid evil, and race with one another in doing good. They are ňĻtrulyňļ among the righteous.
They will never be denied the reward for any good they have done. And Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of those mindful ňĻof Himňļ.
Indeed, neither the wealth nor children of the disbelievers will be of any benefit to them against Allah. It is they who will be the residents of the Fire. They will be there forever.
The good they do in this worldly life is like the harvest of an evil people struck by a bitter wind, destroying it ňĻcompletelyňļ. Allah never wronged them, but they wronged themselves.

O believers! Do not associate closely with others who would not miss a chance to harm you. Their only desire is to see you suffer. Their prejudice has become evident from what they say‚ÄĒand what their hearts hide is far worse. We have made Our revelations clear to you, if only you understood.

Here you are! You love them but they do not love you, and you believe in all Scriptures. When they meet you they say, ‚ÄúWe believe.‚ÄĚ But when alone, they bite their fingertips in rage. Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúňĻMay youňļ die of your rage!‚ÄĚ Surely Allah knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.

When you ňĻbelieversňļ are touched with good, they grieve; but when you are afflicted with evil, they rejoice. ňĻYet,ňļ if you are patient and mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, their schemes will not harm you in the least. Surely Allah is Fully Aware of what they do.
ňĻRemember, O Prophet,ňļ when you left your home in the early morning to position the believers in the battlefield. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
ňĻRememberňļ when two groups among you ňĻbelieversňļ were about to cower, then Allah reassured them. So in Allah let the believers put their trust.
Indeed, Allah made you victorious at Badr when you were ňĻvastlyňļ outnumbered. So be mindful of Allah, perhaps you will be grateful.
ňĻRemember, O Prophet,ňļ when you said to the believers, ‚ÄúIs it not enough that your Lord will send down a reinforcement of three thousand angels for your aid?‚ÄĚ
Most certainly, if you ňĻbelieversňļ are firm and mindful ňĻof Allahňļ and the enemy launches a sudden attack on you, Allah will reinforce you with five thousand angels designated ňĻfor battleňļ.
Allah ordained this ňĻreinforcementňļ only as good news for you and reassurance for your hearts. And victory comes only from Allah‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise‚ÄĒ
to destroy a group of the disbelievers and humble the rest, causing them to withdraw in disappointment.
You ňĻO Prophetňļ have no say in the matter. <sup foot_note=156>156</sup> It is up to Allah to turn to them in mercy or punish them, for indeed they are wrongdoers.

foot_notes.156: When the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) was injured at the Battle of UŠł•ud, some of his companions suggested that he should pray against the pagans of Mecca. He responded, ‚ÄúI have not come to condemn people, but as a mercy to pray for their guidance.‚ÄĚ The verse here says that it is not up to the Prophet whether they believe or disbelieve. A prophet‚Äôs only duty is to convey the message. Eventually, almost all Meccans accepted Islam before the death of the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) .

text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. He forgives whoever He wills, and punishes whoever He wills. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
O believers! Do not consume interest, multiplying it many times over. And be mindful of Allah, so you may prosper.
Guard yourselves against the Fire prepared for the disbelievers.
Obey Allah and the Messenger, so you may be shown mercy.
And hasten towards forgiveness from your Lord and a Paradise as vast as the heavens and the earth, prepared for those mindful ňĻof Allahňļ.
ňĻThey areňļ those who donate in prosperity and adversity, control their anger, and pardon others. And Allah loves the good-doers.
ňĻThey areňļ those who, upon committing an evil deed or wronging themselves, remember Allah and seek forgiveness and do not knowingly persist in sin‚ÄĒand who forgives sins except Allah?
Their reward is forgiveness from their Lord and Gardens under which rivers flow, staying there forever. How excellent is the reward for those who work ňĻrighteousnessňļ!
Similar situations came to pass before you, so travel throughout the land and see the fate of the deniers.
This <sup foot_note=157>157</sup> is an insight to humanity‚ÄĒa guide and a lesson to the God-fearing.

foot_notes.157: ‚ÄúThis‚ÄĚ either refers to how the forces of evil are ultimately destroyed, or that the Quran is a reminder of the destruction of evildoers.

text: Do not falter or grieve, for you will have the upper hand, if you are ňĻtrueňļ believers.

If you have suffered injuries ňĻat UŠł•udňļ, they suffered similarly ňĻat Badrňļ. We alternate these days ňĻof victory and defeatňļ among people so that Allah may reveal the ňĻtrueňļ believers, choose martyrs from among you‚ÄĒand Allah does not like the wrongdoers‚ÄĒ
and distinguish the ňĻtrueňļ believers and destroy the disbelievers.
Do you think you will enter Paradise without Allah proving which of you ňĻtrulyňļ struggled ňĻfor His causeňļ and patiently endured?
You certainly wished ňĻfor the opportunityňļ for martyrdom before encountering it, now you have seen it with your own eyes.

MuŠł•ammad is no more than a messenger; other messengers have gone before him. If he were to die or to be killed, would you regress into disbelief? Those who do so will not harm Allah whatsoever. And Allah will reward those who are grateful.

No soul can ever die without Allah‚Äôs Will at the destined time. Those who desire worldly gain, We will let them have it, and those who desire heavenly reward, We will grant it to them. And We will reward those who are grateful.

ňĻImagineňļ how many devotees fought along with their prophets and never faltered despite whatever ňĻlossesňļ they suffered in the cause of Allah, nor did they weaken or give in! Allah loves those who persevere.
And all they said was, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Forgive our sins and excesses, make our steps firm, and grant us victory over the disbelieving people.‚ÄĚ
So Allah gave them the reward of this world and the excellent reward of the Hereafter. For Allah loves the good-doers.
O believers! If you yield to the disbelievers, they will drag you back to disbelief‚ÄĒand you will become losers.
But no! Allah is your Guardian, and He is the best Helper.

We will cast horror into the hearts of the disbelievers for associating ňĻfalse godsňļ with Allah‚ÄĒa practice He has never authorized. The Fire will be their home‚ÄĒwhat an evil place for the wrongdoers to stay!

Indeed, Allah fulfilled His promise to you when you ňĻinitiallyňļ swept them away by His Will, then your courage weakened and you disputed about the command and disobeyed, <sup foot_note=159>159</sup> after Allah had brought victory within your reach. Some of you were after worldly gain while others desired a heavenly reward. He denied you victory over them as a test, yet He has pardoned you. And Allah is Gracious to the believers.

foot_notes.159: The archers disputed whether to keep their positions after the initial victory. Eventually, most of them decided to go in pursuit of the spoils of war, disobeying the Prophet‚Äôs direct orders not to leave their position no matter what happened. Defeat became inevitable.

text: ňĻRememberňļ when you were running far away ňĻin panicňļ‚ÄĒnot looking at anyone‚ÄĒwhile the Messenger was calling to you from behind! So Allah rewarded your disobedience with distress upon distress. Now, do not grieve over the victory you were denied or the injury you suffered. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

Then after distress, He sent down serenity in the form of drowsiness overcoming some of you, while others were disturbed by evil thoughts about Allah‚ÄĒthe thoughts of ňĻpre-Islamicňļ ignorance. They ask, ‚ÄúDo we have a say in the matter?‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúAll matters are destined by Allah.‚ÄĚ They conceal in their hearts what they do not reveal to you. They say ňĻto themselvesňļ, ‚ÄúIf we had any say in the matter, none of us would have come to die here.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúEven if you were to remain in your homes, those among you who were destined to be killed would have met the same fate.‚ÄĚ Through this, Allah tests what is within you and purifies what is in your hearts. And Allah knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.

Indeed, those ňĻbelieversňļ who fled on the day when the two armies met were made to slip by Satan because of their misdeeds. But Allah has pardoned them. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Forbearing.

O believers! Do not be like the unfaithful <sup foot_note=160>160</sup> who say about their brothers who travel throughout the land or engage in battle, ‚ÄúIf they had stayed with us, they would not have died or been killed.‚ÄĚ Allah makes such thinking a cause of agony in their hearts. It is Allah who gives life and causes death. And Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.
Should you be martyred or die in the cause of Allah, then His forgiveness and mercy are far better than whatever ňĻwealthňļ those ňĻwho stay behindňļ accumulate.
Whether you die or are martyred‚ÄĒall of you will be gathered before Allah.

It is out of Allah‚Äôs mercy that you ňĻO Prophetňļ have been lenient with them. Had you been cruel or hard-hearted, they would have certainly abandoned you. So pardon them, ask Allah‚Äôs forgiveness for them, and consult with them in ňĻconductingňļ matters. Once you make a decision, put your trust in Allah. Surely Allah loves those who trust in Him.
If Allah helps you, none can defeat you. But if He denies you help, then who else can help you? So in Allah let the believers put their trust.

It is not appropriate for a prophet to illegally withhold spoils of war. And whoever does so, it will be held against them on the Day of Judgment. Then every soul will be paid in full for what it has done, and none will be wronged.
Are those who seek Allah‚Äôs pleasure like those who deserve Allah‚Äôs wrath? Hell is their home. What an evil destination!
They ňĻeachňļ have varying degrees in the sight of Allah. And Allah is All-Seeing of what they do.

Indeed, Allah has done the believers a ňĻgreatňļ favour by raising a messenger from among them‚ÄĒreciting to them His revelations, purifying them, and teaching them the Book and wisdom. For indeed they had previously been clearly astray.

Why is it when you suffered casualties ňĻat UŠł•udňļ‚ÄĒalthough you had made your enemy suffer twice as much ňĻat Badrňļ‚ÄĒyou protested, ‚ÄúHow could this be?‚ÄĚ? Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIt is because of your disobedience.‚ÄĚ Surely Allah is Most Capable of everything.
So what you suffered on the day the two armies met was by Allah‚Äôs Will, so that He might distinguish the ňĻtrueňļ believers

and expose the hypocrites. When it was said to them, ‚ÄúCome fight in the cause of Allah or ňĻat leastňļ defend yourselves,‚ÄĚ they replied, ‚ÄúIf we had known there was fighting, we would have definitely gone with you.‚ÄĚ They were closer to disbelief than to belief on that day‚ÄĒfor saying with their mouths what was not in their hearts. Allah is All-Knowing of what they hide.

Those who sat at home, saying about their brothers, ‚ÄúHad they listened to us, they would not have been killed.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúTry not to die <sup foot_note=161>161</sup> if what you say is true!‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.161: i.e., try not to die when your time comes.

text: Never think of those martyred in the cause of Allah as dead. In fact, they are alive with their Lord, well provided for‚ÄĒ
rejoicing in Allah‚Äôs bounties and being delighted for those yet to join them. There will be no fear for them, nor will they grieve.
They are joyful for receiving Allah‚Äôs grace and bounty, and that Allah does not deny the reward of the believers.

ňĻAs forňļ those who responded to the call of Allah and His Messenger after their injury, <sup foot_note=162>162</sup> those of them who did good and were mindful ňĻof Allahňļ will have a great reward.

foot_notes.162: The Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) realized that the city of Medina became vulnerable after the Muslim loss at UŠł•ud. So on the next day of the battle he decided to lead a small force of his companions‚ÄĒmany of whom had been wounded at UŠł•ud‚ÄĒto chase away the Meccan army which was camping at a place called Šł§amr√Ę' Al-Asad‚ÄĒnot far from Medina. Abu Sufy√Ęn, commander of the Meccan army, sent a man to discourage the Muslims from following the Meccans. Although the man falsely claimed that the Meccans were mobilizing to launch a decisive attack on Medina, the Prophet became more determined to chase them away. Eventually, the Meccans decided to flee and not waste their victory after the Prophet sent a revert to Islam‚ÄĒwho was friends with Abu Sufy√Ęn‚ÄĒto convince him to withdraw; otherwise Muslims were going to avenge their loss at UŠł•ud.

text: Those who were warned, ‚ÄúYour enemies have mobilized their forces against you, so fear them,‚ÄĚ the warning only made them grow stronger in faith and they replied, ‚ÄúAllah ňĻaloneňļ is sufficient ňĻas an aidňļ for us and ňĻHeňļ is the best Protector.‚ÄĚ
So they returned with Allah‚Äôs favours and grace, suffering no harm. For they sought to please Allah. And surely Allah is ňĻtheňļ Lord of infinite bounty.
That ňĻwarningňļ was only ňĻfromňļ Satan, trying to prompt you to fear his followers. <sup foot_note=163>163</sup> So do not fear them; fear Me if you are ňĻtrueňļ believers.

foot_notes.163: This refers to the warning mentioned in 3:173.

text: ňĻO Prophet!ňļ Do not grieve for those who race to disbelieve‚ÄĒsurely they will not harm Allah in the least. It is Allah‚Äôs Will to disallow them a share in the Hereafter, and they will suffer a tremendous punishment.
Those who trade belief for disbelief will never harm Allah in the least, and they will suffer a painful punishment.
Those who disbelieve should not think that living longer is good for them. They are only given more time to increase in sin, and they will suffer a humiliating punishment.

Allah would not leave the believers in the condition you were in, until He distinguished the good from the evil ňĻamong youňļ. Nor would Allah ňĻdirectlyňļ reveal to you the unseen, <sup foot_note=164>164</sup> but He chooses whoever He wills as a messenger. So believe in Allah and His messengers. And if you are faithful and mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, you will receive a great reward.

foot_notes.164: i.e., the distinction between the true believers and hypocrites.

text: And do not let those who ňĻgreedilyňļ withhold Allah‚Äôs bounties think it is good for them‚ÄĒin fact, it is bad for them! They will be leashed ňĻby their necksňļ on the Day of Judgment with whatever ňĻwealthňļ they used to withhold. And Allah is the ňĻsoleňļ inheritor of the heavens and the earth. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

Indeed, Allah has heard those ňĻamong the Jewsňļ who said, ‚ÄúAllah is poor; we are rich!‚ÄĚ We have certainly recorded their slurs and their killing of prophets unjustly. Then We will say, ‚ÄúTaste the torment of burning!
This is ňĻthe rewardňļ for what your hands have done. And Allah is never unjust to ňĻHisňļ creation.‚ÄĚ

Those ňĻare the same peopleňļ who say, ‚ÄúAllah has commanded us not to believe in any messenger unless he brings us an offering to be consumed by fire ňĻfrom the skyňļ.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúOther prophets did in fact come to you <sup foot_note=165>165</sup> before me with clear proofs and ňĻevenňļ what you demanded‚ÄĒwhy then did you kill them, if what you say is true?‚ÄĚ
If you are rejected by them, so too were messengers before you who came with clear proofs, divine Books, and enlightening Scriptures. <sup foot_note=166>166</sup>

foot_notes.166: i.e., the original Torah of Moses and the Gospel of Jesus.

text: Every soul will taste death. And you will only receive your full reward on the Day of Judgment. Whoever is spared from the Fire and is admitted into Paradise will ňĻindeedňļ triumph, whereas the life of this world is no more than the delusion of enjoyment.

You ňĻbelieversňļ will surely be tested in your wealth and yourselves, <sup foot_note=167>167</sup> and you will certainly hear many hurtful words from those who were given the Scripture before you and ňĻfromňļ the polytheists. But if you are patient and mindful ňĻof Allahňļ‚ÄĒsurely this is a resolve to aspire to.

foot_notes.167: Meaning, a believer is always tested through the loss of wealth as well as sickness, injuries, and the loss of life.

text: ňĻRemember, O Prophet,ňļ when Allah took the covenant of those who were given the Scripture to make it known to people and not hide it, yet they cast it behind their backs and traded it for a fleeting gain. What a miserable profit!
Do not let those who rejoice in their misdeeds and love to take credit for what they have not done think they will escape torment. They will suffer a painful punishment.
To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. And Allah is Most Capable of everything.
Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of the day and night there are signs for people of reason.

ňĻThey areňļ those who remember Allah while standing, sitting, and lying on their sides, and reflect on the creation of the heavens and the earth ňĻand prayňļ, ‚ÄúOur Lord! You have not created ňĻall ofňļ this without purpose. Glory be to You! Protect us from the torment of the Fire.
Our Lord! Indeed, those You commit to the Fire will be ňĻcompletelyňļ disgraced! And the wrongdoers will have no helpers.

Our Lord! We have heard the caller <sup foot_note=168>168</sup> to ňĻtrueňļ belief, ňĻproclaiming,ňļ ‚ÄėBelieve in your Lord ňĻaloneňļ,‚Äô so we believed. Our Lord! Forgive our sins, absolve us of our misdeeds, and allow us ňĻeachňļ to die as one of the virtuous.
Our Lord! Grant us what You have promised us through Your messengers and do not put us to shame on Judgment Day‚ÄĒfor certainly You never fail in Your promise.‚ÄĚ

So their Lord responded to them: ‚ÄúI will never deny any of you‚ÄĒmale or female‚ÄĒthe reward of your deeds. Both are equal in reward. Those who migrated or were expelled from their homes, and were persecuted for My sake and fought and ňĻsomeňļ were martyred‚ÄĒI will certainly forgive their sins and admit them into Gardens under which rivers flow, as a reward from Allah. And with Allah is the finest reward!‚ÄĚ
Do not be deceived by the prosperity of the disbelievers throughout the land.
It is only a brief enjoyment. Then Hell will be their home‚ÄĒwhat an evil place to rest!
But those who are mindful of their Lord will be in Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there forever‚ÄĒas an accommodation from Allah. And what is with Allah is best for the virtuous.

Indeed, there are some among the People of the Book who truly believe in Allah and what has been revealed to you ňĻbelieversňļ and what was revealed to them. They humble themselves before Allah‚ÄĒnever trading Allah‚Äôs revelations for a fleeting gain. Their reward is with their Lord. Surely Allah is swift in reckoning.
O believers! Patiently endure, persevere, stand on guard, <sup foot_note=169>169</sup> and be mindful of Allah, so you may be successful.

foot_notes.169: R√ĘbiŠĻ≠o refers to watching over the borders of one‚Äôs homeland. It can also mean keeping up one‚Äôs prayers.
